beats: The great clock shudders, hiccups, and stops — at 3:59. One minute BEFORE teatime. Forever reachable, never past.
The Baron is escorted down the funicular, complaining about the seating arrangements. Mr. Fondant asks, very quietly, if he might keep the wrapper.
"Not bad, Grey," says Penny, pocketing the silver olive pick. "Next time, I get the tuxedo and you carry the biscuits."
Somewhere in London, Auntie pours. It is exactly four o'clock, and everything is possible.
